pytorch.reason: Construct L/R spinor tensors on S³; compute inner product matrix to verify chirality orthogonality

pyg.reason: not required for this shell-stacking test

z3.reason: Encode chirality winding coefficient impossibility: no λ≠0 satisfies λ=-λ (UNSAT)

cvc5.reason: not required; z3 is sufficient for the chirality scalar impossibility proof

sympy.reason: Symbolic 4×4 γ-matrix algebra to verify P_L·P_R=0 and idempotency of chirality projectors

clifford.reason: Cl(3) bivector e12/e21 algebra for spinor construction and chirality encoding

geomstats.reason: S³ geodesic distance confirms L/R geometric connection (min_dist=0 at ξ=0); corrects β0=2 claim

e3nn.reason: not required; Cl(3) bivectors directly encode chirality without e3nn irreps

rustworkx.reason: PyDAG to encode and verify the shell constraint hierarchy: S3 → Hopf → Weyl

xgi.reason: not required; shell hierarchy is a simple DAG, not a hypergraph

toponetx.reason: not required; GUDHI persistence directly measures β0/β1

gudhi.reason: AlphaComplex persistent homology to measure β0/β1 for shell-stacking claim

hopf_L_fiber.note: L-only Hopf fiber is one U(1) circle: β0=1, β1=1

weyl_LR_combined.corrected_claim: beta0=1 (connected wedge S1 v S1); chirality is projector-level, not geometric
weyl_LR_combined.note: L+R spinors share the ξ=0 basepoint; combined topology is wedge, NOT disjoint tori

z3_chirality_unsat.note: No nonzero λ satisfies λ = -λ; chirality classes cannot smoothly interpolate

geomstats_geodesic.old_wrong_interpretation: geodesic > 0 was taken as chirality barrier; actually just generic angle separation
geomstats_geodesic.corrected_claim: min_dist=0 at ξ=0 CONFIRMS geometric connection; chirality is projector-level only
geomstats_geodesic.note: L and R spinors touch at ξ=0 on S³; no geometric disconnection; projector P_L*P_R=0 is the algebraic separation

sympy_projectors.note: Chirality projectors are orthogonal (P_L·P_R=0), idempotent, and complete

rustworkx_shell_dag.note: Shell hierarchy DAG: S3 → Hopf → Weyl encodes constraint stacking order; Weyl shell is projector restriction (β0=1), not geometric disconnection

pytorch_spinors.old_wrong_condition: abs(mean_inner) < 0.1 (incorrect; mean is negative due to antipodal structure)
pytorch_spinors.corrected_claim: max_inner ≈ 1 (touch at ξ=0 confirmed); mean is negative but that's geometric structure, not disconnection
pytorch_spinors.note: L and R spinor tensors touch at ξ=0 (max_inner=1); chirality is projector-level, not geometric gap

neg_L_only_not_disconnected.note: L-only fiber is a connected U(1) circle (β0=1); CORRECTED: adding R does NOT create β0=2 either

neg_R_only_not_disconnected.note: R-only fiber is a connected U(1) circle (β0=1); CORRECTED: L+R combined is also β0=1 (wedge)

neg_z3_algebraic_sat.note: Algebraically λ=-1 satisfies e12=λ·e21 (SAT); the UNSAT is the topological winding claim

neg_pl_sq_not_zero.note: P_L² = P_L ≠ 0 (projector is non-trivial — negative test confirms idempotency, not annihilation)

boundary_xi_zero_coincide.note: At ξ=0 both chiralities are identical (degenerate point)

boundary_xi_pi_antipodal.note: At ξ=π, L and R spinors are antipodal on S³ (inner product = -1)

boundary_sampling_density.old_wrong_claim: beta0=2 should be robust across densities
boundary_sampling_density.corrected_claim: beta0=1 (connected wedge) should be robust across densities
boundary_sampling_density.note: L+R combined is always β0=1 (wedge S¹∨S¹); β0=2 only for truly disconnected clouds
